methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $56M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Formic is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Saman Farid and Misa Ilkhechi that has raised $56M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs a comparable universe of nine private and one public company peers operating in the same sector, including Foundation Robotics, 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and Collaborative Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($56M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding stage, implies a valuation of $112M. Peer-median analysis draws from seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.6B, though the peer set exhibits wide dispersion (range: $300M to $10.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in product maturity and capital deployment across the robotics subsector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $95M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median estimate of $881M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Formic's technology, market position, and execution trajectory align with higher-valuation peers like Foundation Robotics ($3.0B) or lower-valuation comparables. Key uncertainties include product-market fit evidence, revenue traction relative to peers, and near-term funding runway requirements.
